Recruiter sent me a 'test' from Filtered.ai.Automated pre interviews: answer questions on a webcam and take a skill test. F that.
Forget that he said it was JavaScript but ended up being Node.js.2 minutes in I realized that Filtered's service is BS.
I'm not against automation, but it's trying to replace the very person who sent it to me (and has known me for years).
The Q's were rote, "Tell me about a challenge..."Gross.And you had to leave your webcam on for the code-writing test.To me, that's creepy
Not to mention it rang a few alarms from a security standpoint. What else are they monitoring? Maybe nothing BUT why leave the webcam on?
If not for 'big brother' reasons, I can only conclude their code is shit / unfinished.
